title: Peacocks Take Over a California Town!
summary: Have you ever seen a peacock in your backyard? Probably not, unless you live in Arcadia, California! This town has dozens of peacocks that walk around like they own the place. They strut on rooftops, peck at cars, and call loudly from trees. During mating season, the males show off their amazing colorful feathers. But not everyone is happy about it. Some people love the beautiful birds, while others think they're a big problem. The peacocks were brought to Arcadia over 100 years ago by a rich businessman named Elias Lucky Baldwin. Back then, the area was open land, but now it's full of houses and stores. Some people want to protect the peacocks, and others want to send them away. Filmmaker Callie Barlow made a short movie called "Our Neighbors, the Peacocks" about this strange situation. The movie shows both sides of the story. One person in the movie says living with the birds might mean "putting aside your own discomforts to find a deeper meaning in nature." So, would you want a peacock as a neighbor?
